skill.name: deslop
skill.description: Remove AI-generated code slop from a branch. Use when cleaning up AI-generated code, removing unnecessary comments, defensive checks, or type casts. Checks diff against main and fixes style inconsistencies.
skill.summary: Remove AI-generated code slop from a branch. Use when cleaning up AI-generated code, removing unnece...
skill.author: getsentry

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported backtick execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown text. The skill does instruct the agent to run a read-only git diff command, so it retains a low external command risk factor.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 14. This line is Markdown inline text describing TypeScript casts to any, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside SKILL.md prose and uses Markdown backticks for formatting. No executable script, Ruby code, or shell interpolation exists at this location.

low_findings.title: Read-Only Git Command Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to run git diff main...HEAD. This is an external command instruction, but it is read-only and used to inspect branch changes for code cleanup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present in the skill instructions and is a real external command. The risk is low because git diff reads repository state and does not modify files or access the network.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 2 and 3. The alerts appear to come from the word deslop, which contains the substring des, not from cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only skill metadata and Markdown instructions. No cryptographic API, cipher configuration, key handling, or encryption logic is present.

content.value_statement: AI-assisted branches can collect noisy comments, defensive code, and style drift. This skill reviews the branch diff and removes cleanup issues while preserving intended changes.
actual_capabilities: Compares the current branch against main with git diff.
Identifies comments that do not match local code style.
Finds unnecessary defensive checks and try catch blocks.
Flags casts to any used to bypass type problems.
Moves inline Python imports to the top of the file.
Reports a short summary of cleanup changes.
limitations: Requires a Git repository with a main branch reference.
Does not define automated tests or validation commands.
Relies on the agent to judge local code style correctly.
May need human review for intentional defensive logic.

use_cases.title: Pre-Review Branch Cleanup
use_cases.description: Remove AI-generated noise before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Maintainer Style Review
use_cases.description: Check contributed changes for comments, casts, and defensive code that do not match project norms.

use_cases.title: AI Pairing Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Clean a branch after using Claude, Codex, or Claude Code for implementation work.

prompt_templates.title: Clean My Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deslop on this branch. Review the diff against main and remove AI-generated cleanup issues while preserving behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deslop and focus on comments added in this branch. Remove comments that do not match nearby code style.

prompt_templates.title: Review Defensive Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Check unnecessary guards and try catch blocks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deslop to inspect the branch diff. Remove abnormal defensive checks only when surrounding code shows trusted inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Cleanup Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deslop across the branch. Check comments, any casts, defensive blocks, Python inline imports, and style drift, then summarize changes.

output_examples.input: Clean a branch with extra comments and redundant guards.
output_examples.output: Removed noisy implementation comments and a redundant trusted-path guard. Preserved the functional change and reported the cleanup.

output_examples.input: Review a TypeScript change that used broad type casts.
output_examples.output: Replaced unnecessary any casts with a clearer typed flow where supported by the surrounding code.

output_examples.input: Check a Python file with an import inside a function.
output_examples.output: Moved the inline import to the module import section and kept the behavior unchanged.

best_practices: Run the skill after implementation and before final review.
Review each removal to confirm it does not change behavior.
Run project tests after cleanup when tests are available.
anti_patterns: Do not remove defensive code that protects untrusted inputs.
Do not use the skill as a replacement for functional review.
Do not clean files outside the branch diff without a clear reason.

faq.question: What does this skill remove?
faq.answer: It removes AI-generated cleanup issues such as noisy comments, abnormal defensive checks, broad any casts, and inline Python imports.

faq.question: Does it change program behavior?
faq.answer: The instructions say to preserve legitimate changes. Users should still run tests after cleanup.

faq.question: Why does it compare against main?
faq.answer: The diff against main limits review to changes introduced by the current branch.

faq.question: Is the git command dangerous?
faq.answer: The listed git diff command is read-only. It inspects changes and does not modify files.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: When should I avoid using it?
faq.answer: Avoid automatic removal when defensive checks handle untrusted input or enforce security boundaries.
